name: Waste Management Challenges
definition: Chronic challenges related to inadequate waste collection, disposal, and recycling infrastructure that create public health, environmental, and climate resilience risks.

name: Inadequate Basic Municipal Services
rationale: Too broad; already covered by rcc.infrastructure_failure when chronic.

name: Waste Management
definition: Actions related to collection, disposal, recycling, and treatment of solid waste including landfill management, waste reduction strategies, composting programs, and circular economy initiatives. Encompasses both infrastructure for waste processing and service delivery for waste collection.
rationale: No clear climate adaptation or resilience application.

name: Waste Management Systems
definition: Challenges related to municipal solid waste collection, recycling infrastructure, composting systems, and waste reduction programs. Includes inadequate waste management capacity, lack of recycling facilities, and food waste management.
rationale: no clear climate adaptation and resilience application

name: Housing Vulnerability / Inadequate Housing
definition: Risks associated with substandard housing conditions, precarious settlements, and lack of climate-resilient housing for vulnerable populations. Includes need for housing improvements, building code updates, and social housing programs.
rationale: not a climate hazard, should be covered in a vulnerability vocabulary

name: Sustainable Mobility Infrastructure
definition: Gaps in low-carbon transportation infrastructure including bicycle lanes, pedestrian pathways, and public transit systems. Addresses need for alternatives to motorized vehicle dependence and integration with green infrastructure.
rationale: not clear enough connection to adaptation and resilience

name: Agriculture & Food Systems
definition: Climate adaptation actions focused on agricultural practices, crop management, livestock improvement, paludiculture, and food production systems. Includes technological interventions (crop varieties, genetic techniques), behavioral changes (cropping practices, agroecology), and agricultural extension services.

name: Agricultural Finance & Risk Management
definition: Economic and financial mechanisms specifically for agricultural climate risk management, including crop insurance schemes, weather-indexed insurance, subsidies, and financial incentives for climate-resilient farming practices.

name: Disaster Preparedness and Emergency Response
definition: Actions focused on preparing for and responding to natural disasters and climate emergencies, including civil defense equipment, emergency planning, and incident response capabilities. Distinct from general planning by focusing specifically on acute hazard events.
rationale: this isnt functional, its sectoral. we will accept the new urban sector recommdnation instead to tag these

name: Emergency & Disaster Management
definition: Actions focused on disaster planning, preparedness, emergency response protocols, and scenario exercises for climate-related hazards. Distinct from general governance by emphasizing operational readiness and emergency coordination.
